extraction.one_line_summary: MERLIER Tim won sprint after reconnaissance revealed barrier-side positioning advantage, while CAVENDISH Mark suffered late puncture
tactical_patterns: MERLIER pre-race reconnaissance identified barrier-side opening in final 300m, exploiting positioning gap others missed.
PLANCKAERT Edward uncontested intermediate sprint harvesting secured black jersey stage early, conserving breakaway energy for classification.
EVENEPOEL Remco positioned front in technical finale prioritizing crash avoidance over team sprint duties despite holding GC.

rider_signals.MERLIER Tim: positive: second stage win after course reconnaissance paid dividends, showing superior sprint preparation methodology
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: full bike length deficit despite opening suggests lead-out timing issues persist across multiple stage attempts
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: rear puncture in final 2km indicates positioning risk-taking, possibly compensating for compromised lead-out
rider_signals.KOOIJ Olav: neutral: podium finish without dedicated lead-out reveals raw speed but limited tactical positioning
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: negative: boxed and harried again after Friday win shows vulnerability to technical finales despite power
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: neutral: prioritized safety positioning over GC team responsibilities with 9-second advantage ahead of mountain finale
rider_signals.PLANCKAERT Edward: positive: black jersey tactics via breakaway intermediate sprint control demonstrates classification-focused racing efficiency
